project-cleanup.label: Clean and Format Output
project-cleanup.summary: Use string functions and formatting for clean results.

quiz-code-cleanup.title: Format Customer Names and Phone Numbers

sketch-clean-format-intro.title: Why Clean and Format Output?
sketch-clean-format-intro.bodyMarkdown: When working with SQL, your query results are often shared with others or used in reports. Clean, well-formatted output makes your data easier to read and understand. SQL provides string functions like `TRIM`, `UPPER`, `LOWER`, `REPLACE`, and formatting tools like `printf` (in SQLite) to help you present your data clearly.

sketch-string-functions.title: Common String Functions
sketch-string-functions.bodyMarkdown:
Some useful string functions in SQL include:

- `TRIM(column)`: Removes leading and trailing spaces.
- `UPPER(column)`: Converts text to uppercase.
- `LOWER(column)`: Converts text to lowercase.
- `REPLACE(column, 'old', 'new')`: Replaces occurrences of 'old' with 'new'.
- `printf(format, ...)`: Formats numbers or strings (SQLite-specific).

sketch-formatting-examples.title: Formatting Examples
sketch-formatting-examples.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you have a table with messy customer names and phone numbers. You can use string functions to:

- Remove extra spaces: `TRIM(name)`
- Standardize case: `UPPER(name)` or `LOWER(name)`
- Format phone numbers: `REPLACE(phone, '-', '')` to remove dashes
- Format prices: `printf('%.2f', price)` to show two decimal places

quiz-code-cleanup.title: Format Customer Names and Phone Numbers
quiz-code-cleanup.prompt: Given the `customers_cleanup` table with columns `name` and `phone`, write a SQL query to select the customer name in uppercase (with no leading/trailing spaces) and the phone number with all dashes removed. Name the columns as `clean_name` and `clean_phone`.
quiz-code-cleanup.starterCode:
-- Write your SQL query here
SELECT 
    -- clean_name,
    -- clean_phone
FROM customers_cleanup;
